generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Components
instance IncubatorBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated styrofoam box housing the incubator system for thermal regulation"
]

instance Heatbed : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Primary heating element within the styrofoam box for maintaining temperature"
]

instance Fan : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical fan distributing heated air uniformly inside the styrofoam box"
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Embedded Raspberry Pi system implementing bang-bang control logic for temperature regulation"
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Heating element actuator controlled via bang-bang controller with adjustable power (0-100%)"
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Fan actuator controlling variable speed distribution of heated air (0-100%)"
]

instance BangBangController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi-based bang-bang controller with statechart implementation (LL, UL, H, C parameters)"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal temperature sensor measuring temperature inside the styrofoam box (high-resolution, ±0.1°C)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second internal temperature sensor measuring temperature inside the styrofoam box (high-resolution, ±0.1°C)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External temperature sensor measuring ambient temperature outside the styrofoam box"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C5: Data
instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Periodic temperature data stream from internal/external sensors (low-frequency, ~1-10 seconds)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData, ExternalTemperatureData
]

instance CalibratedTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed temperature data after RabbitMQ synchronization and potential preprocessing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream
]

// C6: Enablers
instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source RabbitMQ message broker for real-time synchronization of PT/DT data flows"
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database for storing real-time visualization data (e.g., InfluxDB)"
]

instance KalmanFilterImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter implementation for state estimation and anomaly detection"
]

instance DifferentialEquationModels : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Behavioral dynamic simulation models using differential equations for thermal prediction"
]

instance CoSimulationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Co-simulation framework for 'what-if' scenarios using historical/real-time data"
]

instance AutomatedCalibrationSimulator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated tracking simulator for Model B calibration via experimental data"
]

instance StatechartDiagram : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Statechart diagram visualizing bang-bang controller logic"
]

// C7: Models/Data
instance ModelAThermal : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified 2-parameter thermal model for predicting temperature inside incubator (insufficient for heatbed inertia)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DifferentialEquationModels
]

instance ModelBThermal : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enhanced 4-parameter thermal model better capturing heatbed thermal inertia and system dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DifferentialEquationModels
]

instance TemperaturePredictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-level thermal prediction model for visualization/optimization purposes"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelBThermal
]

instance RealTimeStateEstimationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Low-level Kalman filter integrated model for real-time state estimation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelBThermal, KalmanFilterImplementation
]

// C8: Services
instance DataStreamEnabler : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data synchronization service using RabbitMQ for PT/DT communication"
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureDataVisualizationService
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated calibration service for Model B using experimental data and tracking simulator"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelRecalibrationAction
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection service using Kalman filter to estimate heatbed temperature and detect deviations"
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyTriggerAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Controller parameter optimization service using co-simulation with historical/real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:enables ParameterTuningAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization service displaying temperature trends via time-series database"
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureDashboard
]

instance ControllerLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Bang-bang controller logic implementation with statechart visualization"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlStrategyAction
]

// C9: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature trend analysis for incubator status monitoring"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected deviations from expected temperature behavior using Kalman filter estimation"
]

instance CalibrationAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Model B calibration accuracy metrics from experimental validation"
]

instance ControllerParameterOptimization : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimized controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) via co-simulation with historical data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelRecalibrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recalibrated Model B parameters during experimental cooling/heating cycles"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyTriggerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggered actions (experimental collection, model update) upon anomaly detection"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TemperatureDashboard : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generated temperature visualization dashboard from time-series database"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C10: Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains IncubatorBox, Heatbed, Fan, RaspberryPiController
]

instance ThermalControlEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor,
    HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, BangBangController
]

// C11: Data Flow Connections
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 - InternalTemperatureData
instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 - InternalTemperatureData
instance ExternalTemperatureSensor - ExternalTemperatureData
instance InternalTemperatureData - TemperatureDataStream
instance ExternalTemperatureData - TemperatureDataStream
instance TemperatureDataStream - CalibratedTemperatureData

instance CalibratedTemperatureData - ModelBThermal
instance CalibratedTemperatureData - RealTimeStateEstimationModel
instance RabbitMQMessageBroker - TemperatureDataStream
instance RabbitMQMessageBroker - ModelCalibrationService
instance RabbitMQMessageBroker - AnomalyDetectionService

instance ModelBThermal - TemperaturePredictionModel
instance ModelBThermal - RealTimeStateEstimationModel
instance DifferentialEquationModels - ModelAThermal
instance DifferentialEquationModels - ModelBThermal

instance ModelBThermal - ModelRecalibrationAction
instance RealTimeStateEstimationModel - AnomalyDetectionService
instance RealTimeStateEstimationModel - AnomalyTriggerAction

instance CoSimulationFramework - OptimizationService
instance AutomatedCalibrationSimulator - ModelCalibrationService
instance TimeSeriesDatabase - VisualizationService
instance StatechartDiagram - BangBangController


instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
    **Control Flows and Transmission:**
    - **Command Types:**
      - **Heating Commands:** Control signals to the **heatbed** (on/off based on temperature thresholds).
      - **Fan Commands:** Control signals to the **fan** (on/off based on temperature thresholds).
      - **State Updates:** Control states (e.g., heating duration `H`, waiting duration `C`) sent via RabbitMQ (Document 6).

    **Control Message Formats:**
    - **Protocol:** RabbitMQ message broker (asynchronous, decoupled).
    - **Validation/Authentication:**
      - **Safe Protocol:** Implicitly enforced by the **low-level driver** to ensure safe execution of commands (Document 6).
      - **No explicit authentication mechanism** mentioned; assumed to rely on RabbitMQ’s built-in security (e.g., TLS, user permissions).

    **Feedback Systems:**
    - **Feedback Loop:** The **controller** (Raspberry Pi) listens to temperature measurements from sensors and sends commands back to the **low-level driver** via RabbitMQ (Document 6).
    - **Statechart Validation:** Control logic validated using a **statechart** (Figure 8, Document 6) with parameters:
      - **LL (Lower Limit):** Temperature threshold below which heating is triggered.
      - **UL (Upper Limit):** Temperature threshold above which heating is halted.
      - **H (Heating Duration):** Duration the heatbed operates before stopping.
      - **C (Waiting Duration):** Delay after heating to prevent overshoot.

    **Conflict Resolution:**
    - Implicitly resolved by the **bang-bang controller** logic (Document 6), where commands are issued only when temperature thresholds are crossed.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    - **Real-time visualization**: Achieved via continuous monitoring of controller and low-level driver messages (Document 5).
    - **Sampling frequency**: Periodic temperature data collection by the low-level driver (Document 6) is implied to occur at **<1-second intervals** (inferred from 'periodically reads the temperature data' and 'real-time visualization' requirements).
    - **Latency requirements**: <1-second latency for data exchange between physical and digital components (implied by 'real-time visualization' and 'low-level driver' communication protocol).
    - **Self-adaptation loop timing**:
      - **Detection phase**: Anomalies detected via Kalman filter (Document 5) in near-real-time.
      - **Data collection phase**: Experiment scheduling and data gathering (e.g., cooling/heating phases) likely spans **minutes** (e.g., 5–15 minutes for calibration and parameter reconfiguration).
      - **Model recalibration phase**: Automated calibration of Model B (Document 5) occurs on-demand, potentially within **seconds to minutes** depending on system load.
    - **Temporal granularity**: High-resolution time-series data for visualization and anomaly detection (e.g., per-second granularity for temperature measurements).
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    - **Architecture Patterns**:
      - **CPS-Based DT Framework** (Fig. 1): Combines **modeling**, **simulation**, **monitoring**, and **optimization** with the PT (Document 1).
      - **Layered Design**:
        - **Physical Layer**: Incubator hardware (styrofoam box, sensors, actuators).
        - **Cyber Layer**: Raspberry Pi controller + RabbitMQ communication server.
        - **Digital Layer**: DT models (Model A/B), Kalman filter, co-simulations, and self-adaptation logic.

    - **Component Integration**:
      - **Model-Driven Calibration**: Models (A/B) are calibrated via **experimental data** (Section 3) and updated in real-time (Section 5).
      - **State Estimation**: Kalman filter (Section 5) correlates **measured data** (sensors) with **model predictions** (Model B) to estimate heatbed state.
      - **Controller Co-Simulation**: DT runs **what-if simulations** (Section 5) to optimize controller parameters (e.g., H, C) before reconfiguring the physical controller.

    - **Resource Management**:
      - **RabbitMQ**: Acts as a **centralized message broker** for decoupled communication between PT and DT components (Fig. 6).
      - **Time-Series Database**: Stores real-time data for **visualization** (Section 5).
      - **Self-Adaptation Loop**: Dynamically allocates resources for:
        1. Anomaly detection (Kalman filter).
        2. Data collection (cooling/heating phases).
        3. Model recalibration.
        4. Controller optimization.

    - **System-Wide Coordination**:
      - **Temporal Orchestration**: The **self-adaptation loop** (Section 5) ensures **end-to-end synchronization** between PT and DT:
        - **Step 1**: Anomaly detected → DT triggers experiment.
        - **Step 2**: Data collected → Model B recalibrated.
        - **Step 3**: Kalman filter updated → Controller parameters optimized.
        - **Step 4**: Controller reconfigured → PT operates with new parameters.
      - **Protocol Alignment**: RabbitMQ ensures **consistent message ordering** for DT components to react to PT state changes.
  "
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    **Development and Deployment Phases:**
    1. **Conceptualization & Modeling Phase**:
       - Creation of abstract models (e.g., CAD-like models for physical attributes like styrofoam box dimensions, heatbed specifications, and sensor placements).
       - Development of dynamic models (e.g., differential equations for thermal dynamics) to simulate system behavior under varying conditions.
       - Selection of model abstraction levels (e.g., Model A with 2 free parameters vs. Model B with 4 free parameters for thermal inertia).

    2. **Implementation & Integration Phase**:
       - Deployment of a **Digital Twin (DT)** framework integrating:
         - **Data Collection**: Sensors (2 internal + 1 external temperature sensors) feeding real-time data to the PT.
         - **Models**: Cyber-physical models (e.g., Model B for accurate heatbed inertia prediction) calibrated via automated processes.
         - **Algorithms**: Calibration algorithms (e.g., tracking simulator), Kalman filters for state estimation, and supervisory controllers (e.g., bang-bang controller with parameters: LL, UL, H, C).
         - **Communication**: RabbitMQ message broker for real-time synchronization between PT (Raspberry Pi controller) and DT components.

    3. **Operational & Adaptive Phase**:
       - **Real-time Visualization**: Data streaming to a time-series database (implied via dashboard integration) for monitoring incubator status.
       - **Self-Adaptation Loop** (Future Work):
         - Anomaly detection via Kalman filter triggering:
           a) Data collection experiments (e.g., cooling/heating cycles).
           b) Model recalibration (Model B) and controller reconfiguration.
           c) What-if simulations for parameter optimization.
           d) Closed-loop adaptation (e.g., adjusting controller parameters LL, UL, H, C dynamically).

    **Lifecycle Governance**:
    - Open-source hardware/software availability (GitHub repository) enables community-driven validation/testing.
    - Version control implied via modular DT components (e.g., RabbitMQ decouples PT/DT communication).
    - Transition management: Calibration and model updates are automated (e.g., tracking simulator, Model B recalibration).
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Communication Protocol:**
  - **Primary Protocol:** RabbitMQ (open-source message broker).
  - **Deployment:** RabbitMQ is deployed as a **synchronization and communication server** for the incubator system.
  - **Topology & Architecture:**
    - **Point-to-point communication** between components (low-level driver ↔ controller ↔ RabbitMQ).
    - **Message Broker:** RabbitMQ facilitates asynchronous communication between distributed components.
  **Bandwidth & Latency:**
  - Not explicitly quantified, but RabbitMQ is described as lightweight and suitable for real-time communication.
  **Reliability & Redundancy:**
  - No explicit redundancy mechanisms mentioned, but RabbitMQ supports persistence (queues can be configured to retain messages).
  **Security Protocols:**
  - No explicit mention of security protocols, but RabbitMQ supports authentication and encryption (default configurations can include TLS).
  **Network Infrastructure:**
  - RabbitMQ is deployed **on-premises** (as per document context) and can also be deployed in the cloud.
  **Message Flow:**
  - Sensors → Low-level driver → RabbitMQ → Controller (and vice versa for commands).
  **Data Synchronization:**
  - RabbitMQ ensures message synchronization between distributed components.
  **Event-Driven Communication:**
  - Implemented via RabbitMQ's publish-subscribe model.
  **Protocol Version:** Not specified, but RabbitMQ 3.x+ is commonly used for such applications.
  **Scalability:** RabbitMQ supports horizontal scaling via clustering.
 "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
    **Infrastructure:**
    - **Hosting Platforms:**
      - **Edge:** Raspberry Pi (controller host).
      - **Cloud/On-Premises:** RabbitMQ server (depicted as deployable anywhere).
    - **Computational Requirements:**
      - **Controller:** Lightweight (Raspberry Pi 3/4; ~100MHz CPU usage for control loop).
      - **Data Processing:** RabbitMQ handles message routing; no explicit CPU/memory specs.
    - **Storage Requirements:**
      - **Temporary:** RabbitMQ queues (no persistent storage mentioned; assumed ephemeral).
      - **Historical:** Implicitly stored in time-series databases (e.g., for visualization; not detailed).
    - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
      - **Horizontal Scaling:** RabbitMQ supports multiple nodes (not detailed; assumed for high availability).
      - **Load Balancing:** Not specified; inferred as implicit via RabbitMQ’s distributed queues.
    - **Deployment Models:**
      - **Monolithic:** Low-level driver + controller (Fig. 6).
      - **Microservices:** Implicit (RabbitMQ decouples components; e.g., separate services for calibration, monitoring).
    - **Availability/Reliability:**
      - **Uptime:** Not quantified; assumed >99.9% for critical control (RabbitMQ’s durability).
      - **Redundancy:** No explicit failover; RabbitMQ’s distributed nature could enable it.
    - **Deployment Workflow:**
      - **On-Premises:** RabbitMQ deployed locally (Fig. 6).
      - **Cloud:** RabbitMQ cloud service (e.g., AWS MQ, CloudAMQP).
  "
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
    - **Primary integration technology**: **RabbitMQ** (Document 6) for synchronous communication between:
      - Low-level driver ↔ Controller ↔ Digital Twin services.
    - **Data exchange protocols**:
      - **Message-based**: RabbitMQ AMQP protocol for real-time data synchronization.
      - **Time-series database**: Implied for real-time visualization (Document 5).
    - **Interoperability standards**:
      - **Not explicitly documented**: No mention of industry standards (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or extensible plugin architectures.
      - **Dependencies**:
        - **External systems**: None specified; incubator DT operates in isolation (Documents 3–5).
        - **Enterprise integration**: Not applicable; case study is CPS-specific.
    - **Integration patterns**:
      - **Isolated DT instance**: No cross-DT or enterprise system integration mentioned.
      - **Plugin architecture**: Not supported; RabbitMQ is the sole integration layer.
    "
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
    **Open-Source Framework & Accessibility**:
    - The **Incubator case study** (Document 3, 4, 5) is implemented using **open-source hardware/software** (e.g., Raspberry Pi, sensors, and simulation tools), with publicly available specifications accessible via the **INTO-CPS-Association GitHub repository** ([link referenced](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator)).
    - **Data Governance**:
      - No explicit policies on data ownership are mentioned in the documents, but the open-source nature implies **shared ownership** among contributors and researchers.
      - **Privacy Protection**:
        - The incubator’s data (e.g., temperature readings) is **collected via sensors** but lacks explicit mention of anonymization, encryption, or compliance with regulations like GDPR.
        - The **simulation models** (Document 3) are used for **predictive analysis**, but no details on data storage/processing privacy measures are provided.
    - **Consent Management**:
      - Not applicable in a research/academic context; data is likely used for **scientific validation** without individual consent requirements.
    - **Data Sharing Agreements**:
      - The open-source nature suggests **collaborative sharing** among researchers, but no formal agreements are documented.
    - **Not in Document**: No mention of data encryption, access control for sensitive data, or compliance with specific privacy laws (e.g., HIPAA, CCPA).
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
    **Industry Standards & Compliance**:
    - **Not Explicitly Mentioned in Documents**:
      - No direct references to ISO/IEC standards (e.g., ISO 25010 for DT frameworks).
      - No compliance with Industry 4.0 (e.g., IIoT protocols like OPC UA) or autonomic computing frameworks (e.g., IEEE standards for self-adaptive systems).
      - No certification requirements specified for the incubator DT.

    **Technical Interoperability**:
    - **Open-Source Collaboration**:
      - GitHub repository enables community-driven validation/testing (e.g., Model B calibration accuracy).
    - **Protocol Compliance**:
      - RabbitMQ (message broker) for decoupled PT/DT communication (standardized messaging).
      - Low-level drivers abstract hardware-specific protocols (e.g., sensor/actuator commands).

    **Future Work Implications**:
    - **Modular Design**: Components (e.g., RabbitMQ, Kalman filter) are reusable across similar CPS/DT systems.
    - **Reproducibility**: Open-source hardware/software ensures interoperability with other DT implementations.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
    **Technical Security Measures**:
    - **Cyber-Physical System (CPS) Integration**:
      - The incubator’s **cyber-physical architecture** (Document 5) relies on a **Raspberry Pi** as the central controller, communicating via **networked sensors** (e.g., temperature sensors). No explicit mention of **firewall protection** or **network segmentation** for the CPS.
      - **Threat Mitigation**:
        - **Fault-Tolerant Supervisory Control** (Document 13) is referenced in related research but **not implemented** in the incubator case study.
        - **Self-Adaptive Systems** (Document 15) are mentioned as a related concept, but the incubator lacks **automatic reconfiguration** or **adaptive algorithms** for dynamic threats.
      - **Fail-Safe Mechanisms**:
        - The **insulated styrofoam box** (Document 4) provides **thermal isolation**, but no **hardware redundancy** (e.g., backup sensors/heaters) is described.
        - **Safety-Critical Systems**:
          - The incubator’s **temperature regulation** is controlled via a **manual controller** (Document 4), with no **real-time safety validation** (e.g., emergency shutdown protocols).
          - **Calibration Algorithms** (Document 3) are used to update models but **not linked to security validation** (e.g., integrity checks for sensor data).
      - **Access Control**:
        - No mention of **authentication/authorization** for the Raspberry Pi or sensor data access.
      - **Risk Assessment**:
        - The incubator’s **experimental setup** (Document 4) focuses on **thermal dynamics**, but **cybersecurity risks** (e.g., sensor spoofing, network attacks) are not addressed.
      - **Safety-Critical Considerations**:
        - **Models@Run-Time** (Document 11, 12) are used for **real-time monitoring**, but no **formal verification** (e.g., via **FMI-enabled data brokers**, Document 17) is applied to the incubator’s cyber-physical models.
        - **Autonomic Computing** (Document 9) principles (e.g., self-healing) are **not implemented**; the system relies on manual intervention for adjustments.
    - **Technologies Mentioned for Related Work**:
      - **Formal Methods** (Document 7) and **Self-Adaptive Systems** (Document 15) are cited as enabling technologies for broader DT frameworks but **not deployed** in the incubator.
      - **SciPy** (Document 14) is referenced for **scientific computing** but not for security validation.
      - **Kalman Filters** (Document 16) are used for **state estimation** but lack **security-aware calibration**.
    - **Not in Document**:
      - No mention of **encryption protocols** (e.g., TLS for sensor data), **intrusion detection systems**, or **compliance with cybersecurity standards** (e.g., ISO 27001).
      - No **fail-safe mechanisms** for cyber-attacks (e.g., isolated sensor networks).
      - No **real-time safety monitoring** beyond basic temperature thresholds.
    "
]
